steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence sets the pattern: most discovered planets orbit G-type stars like the Sun. The next sentence introduces KELT-9b, which orbits a B-type star — twice as massive and nearly twice as hot as the Sun. That is the opposite of the typical case, so the relationship is contrast.

steps.title: Pick the contrast transition
steps.content: A contrast between a general pattern and an exception requires a contrast signal. "However" is the standard contrast transition and fits the "most X; but here is an exception" structure exactly.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — likewise,: signals similarity, but the KELT-9 planet contradicts the G-type pattern rather than echoing it. ✗
  • Choice B — however,: signals contrast, which matches the move from typical G-type case to atypical B-type case. ✓
  • Choice C — therefore,: signals a cause-effect conclusion, but the discovery is not a consequence of the prior sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — for example,: would treat KELT-9b as an example of the G-type pattern, but it is the opposite — a B-type star, not a G-type. ✗
That is the correct answer.
